Eye Style Exploration

Ferni Eye Mood Board

Different eye styles inspired by Pixar characters. Each conveys a different personality. Which one feels most like Ferni?

Round Face Styles (Current Direction)

A1: Current Design

Inspired by: Generic Smiley

Large oval eyes with small pupils. Friendly but perhaps too simplistic — lacks the mechanical charm of WALL-E.

SimpleFriendlyGeneric

A2: WALL-E Binocular

Inspired by: WALL-E's Camera Eyes

Layered circular eyes with depth — outer ring, inner ring, iris, pupil. Creates mechanical warmth. The highlight dot adds life.

MechanicalWarmExpressiveDepth

A3: EVE Minimal

Inspired by: EVE (WALL-E)

Horizontal oval eyes, no pupils. Ultra-clean and modern. Perhaps too cold for a life coach — EVE starts cold and warms up.

MinimalModernSleekCool

A4: Cyclops Friendly

Inspired by: Luxo Jr. / Mike Wazowski

Single large eye is incredibly expressive but very distinctive. Makes Ferni unique but might feel too "monster-like".

UniqueExpressiveBoldMemorable

A5: Baymax Connected

Inspired by: Baymax (Big Hero 6)

Simple dots connected by a line. Healthcare robot feel. Very friendly but perhaps too medical/clinical for life coaching.

HealthcareCaringSimpleTrust

A6: Joy Sparkle

Inspired by: Joy (Inside Out)

Large eyes with colored irises and multiple sparkle highlights. Very emotional and expressive. Conveys optimism and warmth.

EmotionalOptimisticWarmSparkly

Alternative Shapes

B1: Rounded Square

Inspired by: App Icons / Robot

Square with rounded corners feels more "app-like" and modern. WALL-E binocular eyes work well here too.

ModernApp-likeTech

B2: Capsule

Inspired by: Minions / Pill Shape

Vertical capsule shape is playful and unique. Works well at small sizes. Has a friendly, approachable feel.

PlayfulUniqueFun

B3: Soft Blob

Inspired by: Organic Forms

Slightly irregular circle feels more organic and alive. Like a character that could squash and stretch naturally.

OrganicAliveSoft

Eye Detail Variations

C1: Heavy Rim

Inspired by: WALL-E + Glasses

Thick dark rims around eyes like binoculars or glasses. Very WALL-E. The rim creates clear eye boundaries.

WALL-EDefinedCharacter

C2: Gradient Iris

Inspired by: Human Eyes + Brand

Green gradient iris matches Ferni's brand color. More human-like and emotional. Creates depth and warmth.

HumanOn-brandEmotionalDepth

C3: Subtle Lids

Inspired by: Animated Characters

Subtle eyelid curves above eyes suggest expression without being heavy. Can convey curiosity, tiredness, focus.

ExpressiveSubtleAnimated

C4: Rectangular Binocular

Inspired by: WALL-E (more literal)

More rectangular binocular shape, closer to actual WALL-E. Very mechanical and robot-like. Strong character.

MechanicalRobotWALL-EStrong

Pixar Character Reference

Key eye design principles from beloved Pixar characters

WALL-E

Binocular-shaped eyes with multiple rings creating depth. Expressiveness comes from tilting and focusing. Mechanical but incredibly warm.

EVE

Simple horizontal ovals, no detail. Emotion conveyed through shape changes (curved = happy, angled = angry). Clean, modern, sleek.

Luxo Jr.

Single lamp "eye" that is the light itself. Proves you can have massive personality with minimal features. Movement is key.

Mike Wazowski

Giant single eye dominates face. Highly expressive through size changes, lid position. Memorable silhouette.

Baymax

Two dots connected by a line. Healthcare aesthetic. Blinking is the main expression. Trust through simplicity.

Joy (Inside Out)

Large eyes with sparkle highlights and colored irises. Maximum emotion and expressiveness. Optimistic, warm, human-like.
